req.title: Rate limiting
req.statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-024 defines a rate limit of 60 requests/minute/user with 429+Retry-After on overage. Four test cases cover: (1) EP-positive: sending 30 requests (well within the 60/min quota) all succeed with 2xx; (2) BVA-boundary: sending exactly 60 requests verifies the last one at the limit is still allowed (2xx), then the 61st is rejected (429); (3) NEG-negative: sending 61+ requests in a burst confirms 429 is returned with a Retry-After header on the overage requests; (4) STC: verifies state transition from rate-limited back to allowed after the retry window elapses. Each test resets rate-limit state via a dedicated fixture that authenticates a fresh ephemeral user or resets a test-scoped counter, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_requests_within_limit_all_succeed
preconditions: A valid authenticated user token exists with a clean request counter (0 requests in current window)
The banking-api is reachable at BASE_URL
Rate-limit window has been reset for this user
actions: Send 30 sequential GET requests to /api/v1/accounts using the user token
Record the HTTP status code of each response
expected: No response contains a Retry-After header
trace: R-FIN-024: requests below the 60/min threshold must succeed
EP: 30 requests is a representative member of the valid partition [1..60]

tests.name: test_61st_request_returns_429_with_retry_after
preconditions: A valid authenticated user token exists with a clean request counter (0 requests in current window)
The banking-api is reachable at BASE_URL
Rate-limit window has been reset for this user
actions: Send 60 GET requests to /api/v1/accounts to exhaust the quota
Send one additional (61st) GET request within the same minute window
Inspect the status code and headers of the 61st response
expected: The 61st response includes a Retry-After header with a positive integer value
trace: R-FIN-024: overages shall return 429 with retry-after
NEG: exercising the rejection path when quota is exceeded by one

tests.name: test_exactly_60th_request_is_allowed
preconditions: A valid authenticated user token exists with a clean request counter (0 requests in current window)
The banking-api is reachable at BASE_URL
Rate-limit window has been reset for this user
actions: Send 59 GET requests to /api/v1/accounts to reach the penultimate position
Send exactly the 60th GET request within the same minute window
Inspect the status code and absence of Retry-After on the 60th response
expected: The 60th response does NOT include a Retry-After header
The boundary value at the limit is inclusive and permitted
trace: R-FIN-024: 60 requests per minute is the inclusive upper bound of allowed requests
BVA: testing the exact boundary value N=60 (on-point) and N-1=59 (off-point)

tests.name: test_rate_limit_resets_after_retry_window
preconditions: A valid authenticated user token exists with a clean request counter (0 requests in current window)
The banking-api is reachable at BASE_URL
Rate-limit window has been reset for this user
actions: Exhaust the quota by sending 60 GET requests to /api/v1/accounts
Confirm the 61st request returns 429 and capture the Retry-After value
Wait for the number of seconds specified in Retry-After (up to 65 s cap)
Send one GET request after the window has elapsed
expected: The 61st request returns 429 confirming the limited state
After waiting Retry-After seconds, the next request returns HTTP 2xx
The system transitions from rate-limited state back to allowed state
trace: R-FIN-024: rate limit is per minute; window must reset allowing subsequent requests
STC: covers the transition limited->allowed after the retry window expires
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

def test_rate_limit_resets_after_retry_window(fresh_user_token, base_url):
    endpoint = f"{base_url}/api/v1/accounts"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {fresh_user_token}"}
    for i in range(60):
        r = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
        assert r.status_code in (200, 201, 204), f"Setup req {i+1}: {r.status_code}"
    limited = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
    assert limited.status_code == 429, (
        f"Expected 429 after quota, got {limited.status_code}"
    )
    retry_after_raw = limited.headers.get("Retry-After", "60")
    wait_sec = min(int(retry_after_raw), 65)
    time.sleep(wait_sec)
    recovered = requests.get(endpoint, headers=headers, timeout=5)
    assert recovered.status_code in (200, 201, 204), (
        f"Expected 2xx after window reset, got {recovered.status_code}"
    )
